summary: Excellent soundboard capture of the July 18‑1990 Deer Creek show that, despite some sloppy playing, delivers a smoking Help > Slipknot! > Franklin’s and strong Terrapin/Other One moments.
review: The SBD source is praised for its clarity and near‑perfect balance, with only occasional mix dips (Phil disappearing briefly). Musically the band was uneven – Jerry and Brent sounded haggard in places, but the Help > Slipknot! > Franklin’s sequence, Terrapin Station, The Other One >Dew and a soaring Morning Dew make this a memorable show. Overall the performance is mixed‑quality, yet the standout jams outweigh the sloppy moments.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers rate the recording highly and note excellent soundboard quality, while opinions on the performance range from sloppy to glowing; the weighted average lands just under 4‑star.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Improved SBD over earlier releases – crisp, balanced and near‑perfect sound. A few reviewers note a brief drop in Phil's bass presence and a flat moment on Franklin’s Tower, but overall the mix is clean and enjoyable.

standout_songs: Help on the Way > Slipknot! > Franklin's Tower
poor_songs: China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider
show_quality.setlist_flow: First set is considered one of the stronger early‑summer sets, while the second set is slightly shorter but still delivers adventurous jamming and a powerful close. The show swings between haggard, sloppy moments and high‑energy peaks.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Often described as sounding haggard or sloppy, yet also noted to be “into it” with hints of Playin' in the Band during transitions.
band_member_comments.Phil: Mix issues cause his bass to dip briefly, but many reviewers say he shines through on this recording and asserts himself during jams like China Cat Sunflower.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent’s keyboards sound tired/haggard; noted for his calliope and carnival B‑3 contributions that add a unique texture despite the overall fatigue.
band_member_comments.Drums: Double‑time feel in parts, intense laughing at the end of the Drum segment, and a generally solid rhythmic foundation.
